title: salix repens subsp. rosmarinifolia
intro: Salix repens subsp. rosmarinifolia, also known as creeping willow or rosemary-leaved willow, is a subspecies of willow tree that belongs to the Salicaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage and promote overall health benefits, such as reducing stress and improving sleep quality. The leaves and bark of Salix repens subsp. rosmarinifolia can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
